DELAYED REPORT. ESTIMATED WINDS BY COOP OBSERVER ALONG THE LAKE SUPERIOR SHORE. ANOTHER GUST OF AROUND 40 MPH OCCURRED AT 1915 EDT AS WELL.
MEASURED AT SAXON HARBOR.
PEAK GUST AT THE WESTERN LAKE SUPERIOR BUOY.
TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
A FEW MAPLE TREES UPROOTED ON LITTLE WEST ROAD. SIZE UNKNOWN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

911 CALL CENTER REPORTED NUMEROUS WITNESSES SAW A WATERSPOUT ON SAWYER LAKE. WITNESSES CLAIM THAT THE WATERSPOUT MOVED ONSHORE TOWARDS WOODLAWN LANE. NUMEROUS TREES ARE DOWNED ALONG WITH POWERLINES NEAR THIS LOCATION. SEVERAL PINE TREES WERE APPROXIMATELY 1 FT IN DIAMETER. DISPATCH ALSO REPORTED THAT THERE WERE 2 TREES THROWN ONTO A HOUSE BY THE WATERSPOUT. TIME IS ESTIMATED BY WITNESSES AND RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED A FOUR MILE PATH OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE ACROSS THE SAWYER AND EDEY LAKE AREA. THERE WAS EVIDENCE OF GUSTNADOES ALONG THE MAIN WIND CORE. STRAIGHT LINE WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 60 TO 70 MPH...WITH POCKETS OF 85 MPH WINDS WITHIN THE EMBEDDED VORTICIES.
MEASURED AT THE BARAGA HANDAR.
PEAK GUST AT THE STANNARD ROCK LIGHTHOUSE.
GRANITE ISLAND OBSERVATION
MEASURED AT THE MARQUETTE COAST GUARD STATION. ANOTHER PEAK WIND GUST TO 29 MPH OCCURRED AT 0512 EDT.
MEASURED AT THE HARVEY RWIS ALONG THE LAKE SUPERIOR SHORE. WIND GUST OF 41 MPH ALSO REPORTED AT 0500 EDT.
TREE DOWN ON COUNTY ROAD 476 SOUTHWEST OF PALMER. SIZE UNKNOWN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

15 INCH DIAMETER BRANCH CAME DOWN ON A COTTAGE ALONG THE NORTHWEST SHORE OF AU TRAIN LAKE. A PERCHED ROOF ON THE PORCH OF THE COTTAGE WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED. OTHER BRANCHES ALSO TOOK OUT A POWER LINE WHICH IS STILL LIVE ON THE GROUND. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TWO 10 TO 12 INCH DIAMETER TREES DOWN AND TENTS BLOWN OVER AT A CAMPGROUND. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

DELAYED REPORT. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE AT THE RIVERSIDE GOLF CLUB. MANY TREES GREATER THAN TWO FEET IN DIAMETER WERE UPROOTED ACROSS THE COURSE. VERY STRONG WINDS OBSERVED BY THE GOLF CLUB STAFF. NO APPARENT DAMAGE TO ANY BUILDINGS. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
DELAYED REPORT. DOZENS OF SNAPPED BRANCHES AND SEVERAL LARGE TREES GREATER THAN 18 INCHES IN DIAMETER FELL ONTO GRAVE MARKERS AT RIVERSIDE CEMETERY.
A FEW 8 INCH DIAMETER TREES WERE SNAPPED. TREES FELL ON POWER LINES CAUSING A POWER OUTAGE. PEA SIZE HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN WERE ALSO REPORTED.
MEASURED AT THE ROCK OF AGES LIGHTHOUSE.
LARGE NUMBER OF TREES DOWNED SURROUNDING THE BENGSTON BRIDGE ON COUNTY ROAD LR. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR. SIZE OF TREES VARIED HOWEVER MANY WERE 8-12 INCHES IN DIAMETER.

TIME WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR. HOLES WERE OBSERVED IN SIDING WITH PILES OF QUARTER SIZED HAIL NEXT TO THE HOUSE 2 HOURS AFTER STORM. HOUSE IS LOCATED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF WILLOW STREET AND EVERGREEN STREET.
MICROBURST WINDS SNAPPED OFF OR UPROOTED NUMEROUS LARGE TREES.
6-7 INCH TREE LIMB DOWNED FROM STORM. ALSO REPORTED LEAVES ALL OVER THE ROAD BUT THEY WERE NOT HOME TO CONFIRM WHETHER IT WAS WIND OR HAIL.
MEASURED AT THE SAXON HARBOR MARINE SITE.
SHIP REPORTED A FEW WATERSPOUTS AND WINDS GUSTING TO 35 KNOTS.
WATER SPOUT REPORTED
MEASURED AT THE MICHIGAN TECH BUOY NEAR THE UPPER ENTRANCE TO THE PORTAGE CANAL.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN. ESTIMATED TO BE 5 TO 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
8 INCH AND 10 INCH DIAMTER TREE DOWN AT A HOTEL. TREE OF UNKNOWN SIZE DOWN ON M-28. POWER OUT IN THE AREA. HAIL HITTING TRUCK...BUT SIZE UNKNOWN DUE TO POOR VISIBILITY.
SPRUCE TREE DOWNED THAT WAS 20 TO 30 FOOT TALL AND 8 INCHES IN DIAMETER. HEAVY WOODEN PICNIC TABLE TOSSED AROUND THE YARD. LAWN CHAIRS BROKEN. NUMEROUS OTHER TREES DOWNED.

TRAILER WEIGHING 300+ POUNDS WAS MOVED 100 FT BY STRONG WINDS. 36 INCH DIAMETER TREES WERE SNAPPED. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. POWER WAS TAKEN OUT. ALSO REPORTED NICKEL SIZE HAIL. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN AROUND THE AREA. THE LARGEST TREE WAS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 40 INCHES IN DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
PEA SIZE HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO 4 CARS FROM HAIL. HAIL SIZE WAS ESTIMATED USING REPORTS IN AREA.
